facts.text: San Francisco was officially renamed from Yerba Buena in 1847, honoring the first civilian pueblo that once called the area good herb.
The Olympic Club, founded in 1860, is the oldest athletic club in the United States and still hosts competitive sports today.
San Francisco’s famous cable cars, a National Historic Landmark, have been whisking tourists up and down hills since the 1870s.
The historic venue that opened in the 1960s became the stage for the Grateful Dead, Janis Joplin, Led Zeppelin, and Jefferson Airplane.
The San Francisco Planning and Urban Research Association, or SPUR, was founded in 1910 to improve housing quality after the earthquake.
Founded in 1855, the University of San Francisco is the city’s oldest higher‑education institution and one of the oldest west of the Mississippi.
